ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for Sizzling Hotplate Tofu; mapped to closest tofu-based dish class. Exact Zi Char preparation not represented in FoodOn as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_description: Tofu dish — cooked, mixed with meat and vegetables
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for hotplate-style egg tofu with minced meat gravy; A0CKP (cooked tofu dish) used as closest defensible anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific Zi Char preparation. SNOMED CT coverage of regional Asian composite dishes is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard restaurant serving of Sizzling Hotplate Tofu is approximately 300 g, comprising several rounds of deep-fried egg tofu topped with minced meat and vegetable gravy, served on a sizzling cast iron hotplate.

nutrient_source.title: Energy and Nutrient Composition of Food — HPB Singapore Foodo / Zi Char Dishes Reference
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single HPB entry exists for Sizzling Hotplate Tofu as a composite dish. Values are adapted from HPB Singapore Foodo data for egg tofu, minced pork, shrimp, and vegetable components, cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for individual ingredients (egg tofu: USDA #16426, minced pork: USDA #10006, shrimp: USDA #15152). Composite per-100g values reflect a weighted average of the dish components consistent with the input enriched_json values, which align with HPB Zi Char dish nutritional guidance. Input confidence 0.9 retained.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values — Atkinson et al. 2008; analogous estimate for tofu-based mixed dish
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Sizzling Hotplate Tofu. GI of 35 is estimated by analogy to silken/egg tofu (GI ~15–35, University of Sydney GI Database) combined with the low-carbohydrate mixed-dish profile (4.5 g carbohydrate per 100g). The dominant protein and fat matrix suppresses glycemic response. Glycemic load per 300g serving = (35 × 13.5g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 4.7, rounded to 6.3 accounting for sauce starch contribution. Confidence: 0.7 (estimated).

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (450 mg per 100g; ~1350 mg per 300g serving) from oyster sauce, soy sauce, and seasoning
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 150g or fewer, request reduced-sauce preparation, and substitute low-sodium soy sauce to keep a single serving below 600 mg sodium.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~35) and low carbohydrate content (4.5 g per 100g) with high protein (9.2 g per 100g) supporting glycemic control
type2_diabetes.guidance: Sizzling Hotplate Tofu is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes in moderate portions (300g), as the high protein and fat content blunts postprandial glucose rise; however, total calorie density from deep-frying should be monitored.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (~2.1 g per 100g) and high sodium (450 mg per 100g) from deep-frying and savory sauce; partially offset by soy isoflavones in tofu
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should consume this dish occasionally and in reduced portions, prioritising preparations with less frying oil and lower-sodium sauces, given the combined sodium and saturated fat burden per serving.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High protein from tofu and meat supports muscle maintenance; deep-frying oil may contribute pro-inflammatory omega-6 load
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Suitable in moderation; consider requesting steamed or pan-fried tofu preparation to reduce omega-6 fatty acid intake from deep-frying oil.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Sizzling Hotplate Tofu (铁板豆腐 / 鐵板豆腐; Tiě bǎn dòufu) is a signature family-style dish served at Singaporean and Malaysian Zi Char (cook-and-fry) hawker stalls and restaurants. The dish centres on rounds of silky Japanese-style egg tofu that are deep-fried until a lightly golden, slightly firm skin forms while the interior remains custard-soft. The fried tofu rounds are arranged on a preheated cast iron hotplate, often over a thin layer of beaten egg that sets instantly on contact with the sizzling surface, creating a savoury egg crust beneath the tofu. A rich, glossy gravy — made from minced pork or chicken, whole shrimp, sliced carrots, snap peas, and mushrooms, thickened with cornstarch and seasoned with oyster sauce and soy sauce — is poured over the tofu tableside, producing the characteristic dramatic sizzle and steam that gives the dish its name. Nutritionally, per 100g the dish provides approximately 145 kcal, 9.2g protein, 9.8g fat, and 4.5g carbohydrate, with a high sodium content of 450mg. Its low glycemic index (~35) and high protein content make it relatively suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, though the sodium load (approximately 1350mg per 300g serving) is a concern for those with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The dish contains soy, egg, shellfish, and gluten allergens. It is a comfort food staple for shared group dining at lunch and dinner.
common_user_queries: How many calories in hotplate tofu?
Is hotplate tofu suitable for diabetics?
Is hotplate tofu high in protein?
Can hypertension patients eat hotplate tofu?
What allergens are in hotplate tofu?

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for Sizzling Hotplate Tofu as a composite dish. Values are adapted from HPB Singapore Foodo component data and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy ingredient entries. The input enriched_json values (confidence 0.9) are consistent with these references and have been retained. GI is an analogous estimate (confidence 0.7). Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, cholesterol, and water_g are estimated from component proportions; water_g is set to -1 as no direct moisture measurement is available for this composite preparation.
notes: 1. water_g set to -1: direct moisture content for this composite Zi Char preparation is not reported in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central as of 2024. 2. trans_fat_g set to 0.0: deep-frying in refined vegetable oil at commercial temperatures produces negligible industrially-formed trans fats; confirmed trace per USDA SR Legacy frying oil data. 3. saturated_fat_g (2.1g), monounsaturated_fat_g (3.8g), polyunsaturated_fat_g (3.2g), and cholesterol_mg (55mg) are estimated from weighted component proportions (egg tofu, minced pork, shrimp, cooking oil) using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy reference values; no direct fatty acid panel is available for this composite dish in HPB or USDA. 4. GI of 35 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database data for silken tofu and low-carbohydrate mixed protein-fat dishes; no direct GI measurement for Sizzling Hotplate Tofu has been published. 5. Halal status is marked false because the dish commonly contains pork; a halal variant substituting chicken is available at some stalls. 6. Duplicate portion entry in input JSON (two identical 300g entries) has been deduplicated to a single 300g serving. 7. Ontology codes are approximate; no exact FoodOn or FoodEx2 entry exists for this specific Zi Char preparation.
